subcommands.name: abort
subcommands.description: Abort the current Graphite command halted by a rebase conflict

name: --force
-f
options.description: Do not prompt for confirmation; abort immediately

name: absorb
ab
subcommands.description: Amend staged changes to the relevant commits in the current stack

name: --all
-a
options.description: Stage all unstaged changes before absorbing

name: --dry-run
-d
options.description: Print which commits the hunks would be absorbed into, but do not actually absorb them

name: --force
-f
options.description: Do not prompt for confirmation; apply the hunks to the commits immediately

name: --patch
-p
options.description: Pick hunks to stage before absorbing

subcommands.name: add
subcommands.description: git add passthrough

subcommands.name: aliases
subcommands.description: Edit your command aliases

options.name: --legacy
options.description: Append legacy aliases to your configuration

options.name: --reset
options.description: Reset your alias configuration

subcommands.name: auth
subcommands.description: Add your auth token to enable Graphite CLI to create and update your PRs on GitHub

name: --token
-t
options.description: Auth token. Get it from: https://app.graphite.dev/activate

name: bottom
b
subcommands.description: Switch to the first branch from trunk in the current stack

subcommands.name: changelog
subcommands.description: Show the Graphite CLI changelog

subcommands.name: cherry-pick
subcommands.description: git cherry-pick passthrough

name: checkout
co
subcommands.description: Switch to a branch. If no branch is provided, opens an interactive selector

name: --all
-a
options.description: Show branches across all configured trunks in interactive selection

name: --show-untracked
-u
options.description: Include untracked branches in interactive selection

name: --stack
-s
options.description: Only show ancestors and descendants of the current branch in interactive selection

name: --trunk
-t
options.description: Checkout the current trunk

subcommands.name: children
subcommands.description: Show the children of the current branch

subcommands.name: completion
subcommands.description: Set up bash or zsh tab completion

subcommands.name: config
subcommands.description: Configure the Graphite CLI

name: continue
cont
subcommands.description: Continues the most recent Graphite command halted by a merge conflict

name: --all
-a
options.description: Stage all changes before continuing

name: create
c
subcommands.description: Create a new branch stacked on top of the current branch and commit staged changes. If no branch name is specified, generate a branch name from the commit message

args.name: name
args.description: The name of the new branch

options.name: --ai
options.description: Automatically AI-generate the branch name and the commit message (if unset)

name: --all
-a
options.description: Stage all unstaged changes before creating the branch, including to untracked files

name: --insert
-i
options.description: Insert this branch between the current branch and its child

name: --message
-m
options.description: Specify a commit message

options.name: --no-ai
options.description: Do not automatically AI-generate the branch name and the commit message. Takes precedence over --ai

name: --patch
-p
options.description: Pick hunks to stage before committing

name: --update
-u
options.description: Stage all updates to tracked files before creating the branch

name: --verbose
-v
options.description: Show unified diff between the HEAD commit and what would be committed at the bottom of the commit message template

subcommands.name: dash
subcommands.description: Open the web dashboard

name: delete
dl
subcommands.description: Delete a branch and its Graphite metadata. Children will be restacked onto the parent branch

args.name: name
args.description: The name of the branch to delete

name: --close
-c
options.description: Close associated pull requests on GitHub

options.name: --downstack
options.description: Also delete any ancestors of the specified branch

name: --force
-f
options.description: Delete the branch even if it is not merged or closed

options.name: --upstack
options.description: Also delete any children of the specified branch

subcommands.name: demo
subcommands.description: Run interactive demos to learn how to use the Graphite CLI

subcommands.name: docs
subcommands.description: Show the Graphite CLI docs

name: down
d
subcommands.description: Switch to the parent of the current branch

args.name: steps
args.description: The number of levels to traverse downstack

name: --steps
-n
options.description: The number of levels to traverse downstack

subcommands.name: feedback
subcommands.description: Post feedback directly to the Graphite maintainers

args.name: message
args.description: Positive or constructive feedback for the Graphite team

name: --with-debug-context
-d
options.description: Include logs from the past 24 hours in your feedback

subcommands.name: fish
subcommands.description: Set up 'fish' tab completion

name: fold
f
subcommands.description: Fold a branch's changes into its parent, update dependencies of descendants of the new combined branch, and restack

name: --keep
-k
options.description: Keeps the name of the current branch instead of using the name of its parent

subcommands.name: freeze
subcommands.description: Freeze specified branch and branches downstack of it. Freezing prevents local modifications including restacks. Undo with gt unfreeze

args.name: branch
args.description: The branch to freeze. Defaults to the current branch

subcommands.name: get
subcommands.description: Sync branches from trunk to the given branch from remote, prompting the user to resolve conflicts. If no branch is provided, sync the current stack

args.name: branch
args.description: Branch or PR number to get from remote

name: --downstack
-d
options.description: When syncing a branch that already exists locally, don't sync upstack branches

name: --force
-f
options.description: Overwrite all fetched branches with remote source of truth

options.name: --restack
options.description: Restack any branches in the stack that can be restacked without conflicts (true by default; skip with --no-restack)

name: --unfrozen
-U
options.description: Checkout new branches as unfrozen (allow local edits)

name: --remote-upstack
-u
options.description: Include upstack PRs when fetching PR information from remote

subcommands.name: guide
subcommands.description: Read extended guides on how to use the gt program

name: info
i
subcommands.description: Display information about the current branch

args.name: branch
args.description: The branch to show info for. Defaults to the current branch

name: --patch
-p
options.description: Show the changes made by each commit

name: --diff
-d
options.description: Show the diff between this branch and its parent. Takes precedence over patch

name: --body
-b
options.description: Show the PR body, if it exists

name: --stat
-s
options.description: Show a diffstat instead of a full diff. Modifies either --patch or --diff. If neither is passed, implies --diff

subcommands.name: init
subcommands.description: Initialize Graphite in this repository by selecting a trunk branch. Can also be used to change the trunk branch of the repository

options.name: --reset
options.description: Untrack all branches

options.name: --trunk
options.description: The name of your trunk branch. If no name is passed, you will be prompted to select one interactively

name: log
l
subcommands.description: Commands that log your stacks

options.name: --classic
options.description: Use the old short logging style, which runs out of screen real estate more quickly. Other options will not work in classic mode

name: --reverse
-r
options.description: Print the log upside down. Handy when you have a lot of branches

name: --show-untracked
-u
options.description: Include untracked branched in interactive selection

name: --stack
-s
options.description: Only show ancestors and descendants of the current branch

name: --steps
-n
options.description: Only show this many levels upstack and downstack. Implies --stack

subcommands.description: Log all branches tracked by Graphite, showing dependencies and info for each

subcommands.name: long
subcommands.description: Display a graph of the commit ancestry of all branches

subcommands.name: short
subcommands.description: Log all stacks tracked by Graphite, arranged to show dependencies

name: merge
mg
subcommands.description: Merge the pull requests associated with all branches from trunk to the current branch via Graphite

name: --confirm
-c
options.description: Asks for confirmation before merging branches. Prompts for confirmation if the local branches differ from remote, regardless of the value of this flag

options.name: --dry-run
options.description: Reports the PRs that would be merged and terminates. No branches are merged

name: modify
m
subcommands.description: Modify the current branch by amending its commit or creating a new commit. Automatically restacks descendants. If you have any unstaged changes, you will be asked whether you'd like to stage them

name: --all
-a
options.description: Stage all changes before committing

name: --commit
-c
options.description: Create a new commit instead of amending the current commit. If this branch has no commits, this command always creates a new commit

name: --edit
-e
options.description: If passed, open an editor to edit the commit message. When creating a new commit, this flag is ignored

options.name: --interactive-rebase
options.description: Ignore all other flags and start a git interactive rebase on the commits in this branch

options.name: --into
options.description: Amend staged changes into a downstack branch

name: --message
-m
options.description: The message for the new or amended commit. If passed, no editor is opened

name: --patch
-p
options.description: Pick hunks to stage before committing

subcommands.name: move
subcommands.description: Rebase the current branch onto the target branch and restack all of its descendants. If no branch is passed in, opens an interactive selector

name: --onto
-o
options.description: Branch to move the current branch onto

options.name: --source
options.description: Branch to move (defaults to current branch)

subcommands.name: parent
subcommands.description: Show the parent of the current branch

subcommands.name: pop
subcommands.description: Delete the current branch but retain the state of files in the working tree

subcommands.name: pr
subcommands.description: Opens the pull request page for a branch or PR number. If no branch is passed, the current branch's PR is opened

args.name: branch
args.description: A branch name or PR number to open

name: rename
rn
subcommands.description: Rename a branch and update metadata referencing it. If no branch name is supplied, you will be prompted for a new branch name. Note that this removes any associated GitHub pull request

name: --force
-f
options.description: Allow renaming a branch that is already associated with an open GitHub pull request

subcommands.name: reorder
subcommands.description: Reorder branches between trunk and the current branch, restacking all of their descendants. Opens an editor where you can reorder branches by moving around a line corresponding to each branch

name: restack
r
subcommands.description: Ensure the current branch is based on its parent, rebasing if necessary. If conflicts are encountered, you will be prompted to resolve them via an interactive Git rebase

options.name: --branch
options.description: Which branch to run this command from. Defaults to the current branch

options.name: --downstack
options.description: Only restack this branch and its ancestors

options.name: --only
options.description: Only restack this branch

options.name: --upstack
options.description: Only restack this branch and its descendants

subcommands.name: revert
subcommands.description: Create a branch that reverts a commit on the trunk branch. Currently experimental

name: --edit
-e
options.description: Edit the commit message

name: split
sp
subcommands.description: Split the current branch into multiple single-commit branches

name: --by-commit
--commit
-c
options.description: Split by commit - slice up the history of this branch

name: --by-hunk
--hunk
-h
options.description: Split by hunk - split into new single-commit branches

name: squash
sq
subcommands.description: Squash all commits in the current branch and restack upstack branches

name: --message
-m
options.description: The updated message for the commit

options.name: --edit
options.description: Modify the existing commit message

name: --no-edit
-n
options.description: Don't modify the existing commit message. Takes precedence over --edit

name: submit
s
subcommands.description: Idempotently force push the current branch to GitHub, creating or updating a pull request.  Opens an interactive prompt that allows you to input pull request metadata. 'gt ss' is a default alias for 'gt submit --stack'

options.name: --always
options.description: Always push updates, even if the branch has not changed. Can be helpful for fixing an inconsistent Graphite stack view on Web/GitHub resulting from downtime/a bug

options.name: --branch
options.description: Which branch to run this command from. Defaults to the current branch

name: --confirm
-c
options.description: Reports the PRs that would be submitted and asks for confirmation before pushing branches and opening/updating PRs. If either of --no-interactive or --dry-run is passed, this flag is ignored

name: --draft
-d
options.description: If set, marks PR as draft. If --no-interactive is true, new PRs will be created in draft mode

options.name: --dry-run
options.description: Reports the PRs that would be submitted and terminates. No branches are pushed and no PRs are opened or updated

name: --edit
-e
options.description: Input metadata for all PRs interactively. If neither --edit nor --no-edit is passed, only prompts for new PRs

name: --force
-f
options.description: Force push: overwrites the remote branch with your local branch. Otherwise defaults to --force-with-lease

name: --merge-when-ready
-m
options.description: If set, marks all PRs being submitted as merge when ready, which will let them automatically merge as soon as all merge requirements are met

name: --no-edit
-n
options.description: Don't edit PR fields inline. Takes precedence over --edit

name: --publish
-p
options.description: If set, publishes PR. If --no-interactive is true, new PRs will be created in draft mode

options.name: --rerequest-review
options.description: Rerequest review from current reviewers

options.name: --restack
options.description: Restack branches before submitting. If there are conflicts, output the branch names that could not be restacked

name: --reviewers
-r
options.description: If set without an argument, prompt to manually set reviewers. Alternatively, accepts a comma separated string of reviewers

name: --stack
-s
options.description: Submit descendants of the current branch in addition to its ancestors

name: --update-only
-u
options.description: Only update the PRs that have been already been submitted

subcommands.name: sync
subcommands.description: Pull the trunk branch from remote and prompt to delete any branches that have been merged. Restacks all branches in your repository that can be restacked without conflicts. If trunk cannot be fast-forwarded to match remote, overwrites trunk with the remote version

options.name: --delete
options.description: Delete merged branches (true by default; skip with --no-delete)

name: --force
-f
options.description: Don't prompt for confirmation before deleting a branch or overwriting trunk to match remote if it can't be fast-forwarded

options.name: --pull
options.description: Pull the trunk branch from remote (true by default; skip with --no-pull)

options.name: --restack
options.description: Restack any branches that can be restacked without conflicts (true by default; skip with --no-restack)

options.name: --show-delete-progress
options.description: Show progress through merged branches

name: top
t
subcommands.description: Switch to the tip branch of the current stack. Prompts if ambiguous

name: track
tr
subcommands.description: Start tracking the current (or provided) branch with Graphite by selecting its parent. This command can also be used to fix corrupted Graphite metadata

name: --parent
-p
options.description: The tracked branch's parent. If unset, prompts for a parent branch

name: --force
-f
options.description: Sets the parent to the most recent tracked ancestor of the branch being tracked. Takes precedence over `--parent`

subcommands.name: trunk
subcommands.description: Switch to the trunk branch

options.name: --add
options.description: Configure an additional trunk branch

args.name: branch
args.description: Branch to add as a trunk

name: untrack
utr
subcommands.description: Stop tracking a branch with Graphite. If the branch has children, they will also be untracked. Default to the current branch if none is passed in

name: --force
-f
options.description: Will not prompt for confirmation before untracking a branch with children

name: up
u
subcommands.description: Switch to the child of the current branch. Prompts if ambiguous

args.name: steps
args.description: The number of levels to traverse upstack

name: --steps
-n
options.description: The number of levels to traverse upstack

subcommands.name: ss
subcommands.description: Default alias for 'submit --stack', submit your changes across all PRs on a stack

subcommands.name: undo
subcommands.description: Undo the most recent Graphite mutations

name: --force
-f
options.description: Do not prompt for confirmation; undo the most recent command immediately

subcommands.name: unfreeze
subcommands.description: Unfreeze a previously frozen branch to allow local modifications

args.name: branch
args.description: The branch to unfreeze. Defaults to the current branch

subcommands.name: unlink
subcommands.description: Unlink a branch from the currently associated pull request

options.name: --version
options.description: Show Graphite CLI version

name: --help
-h
options.description: Show help for Graphite CLI

options.name: --interactive
options.description: Prompt the user. Disable with --no-interactive

options.name: --no-interactive
options.description: Run non interactive

name: --quiet
-q
options.description: Minimize output to the terminal. Implies --no-interactive

options.name: --verify
options.description: Run git hooks. Disable with --no-verify

options.name: --no-verify
options.description: Disable git hooks

options.name: --debug
options.description: Display debug output

options.name: --cwd
options.description: Working directory in which to perform operations
